CheckTitle: Security group restricts PostgreSQL access from the internet
Description: Checks that PostgreSQL TCP port 5432 is not exposed to unrestricted IPv4 (`0.0.0.0/0`) or IPv6 (`::/0`) sources.
Risk: Public PostgreSQL access enables credential attacks and database vulnerability exploitation, risking data disclosure, modification, and outage.

Code.Other: Inspect inbound rules in the ECS console. Locate effective Accept rules from `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0` using TCP or ALL whose actual port range contains PostgreSQL port `5432`. Restrict the source or range, or revoke the exact rule by SecurityGroupRuleId. Revocation using protocol/range/source must exactly match the existing rule.

Recommendation.Text: Do not expose TCP port 5432 to `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
